dialogues.text: The account's been red since Tuesday. I keep moving money between things that are already empty. I'm not asking you to fix it — just a hundred, to make the math lie for one more week.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I've carried this by myself longer than I've let on. I kept thinking you'd notice without me having to say it.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. No — it's fine. I'll figure it out. That's becoming my whole personality lately, figuring it out.

dialogues.text: You've been pale for weeks. I won't ask about it, because every time I do you go somewhere I can't follow. So I'll ask for the hundred instead. That, I can follow.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I'll keep my questions to myself today. I'm getting practiced at it.
dialogues.no_response: I know. It's fine. I said I'd handle it, and I will. I always do.

dialogues.text: I made an appointment I shouldn't have made, and now I can't bring myself to cancel it. A hundred covers the part of it I'm willing to talk about out loud.
dialogues.yes_response: Maybe I'll tell you what it was for when you're back. Maybe I'll have found a way to say it by then.
dialogues.no_response: I'll cancel it. It can wait. Everything in this house has learned how to wait.

dialogues.text: There are things I've meant to say to you for about a year now. This isn't the moment for them. The moment is a hundred dollars, and me pretending that's all this is.
dialogues.yes_response: I'm going to find the words eventually. I will. I just need a little more time than I think I have.
dialogues.no_response: Alright. It can keep. God knows it's kept this long already.

dialogues.text: I called your doctor's office. They told me you should call them back. You never mentioned that to me. I need a hundred, and I need you not to look too closely at my face right now.
dialogues.yes_response: I'm not going to pretend I didn't hear what I heard. But not today. Today I'll just take the hundred.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. Okay. I won't push. I've gotten very good at not pushing.

dialogues.text: I've started sleeping on my own side of the bed again. I don't remember deciding to. A hundred is the easy thing to ask you for tonight, so it's the thing I'm asking.
dialogues.yes_response: I'm not trying to make this about me. I just needed to say it to someone, and you're the only someone I've got.
dialogues.no_response: I know. I know you can't right now. I knew that before I asked.

dialogues.text: I bought something for the house. A lamp, of all things. I thought a warmer light might make the rooms feel less like they're waiting for news. I need the hundred back before it bounces.
dialogues.yes_response: I just wanted one thing to feel ordinary. One stupid, ordinary thing. I thought a lamp might manage it.
dialogues.no_response: I'll return it. The rooms can stay how they are. They were always going to.

dialogues.text: I've been thinking about what comes after. Not in a dark way — in a planning way, which is somehow worse. I'm not ready to say any of it. Just the hundred.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. We'll talk about the rest when there's a version of me that can. There will be one.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. I know you're tired. I can see exactly how tired you are. I know.

dialogues.text: I don't know how to do this. Any of it. There's no manual for the year we're having. A hundred at least fixes the one part of it that comes with a number attached.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I love you. I keep assuming you know, then worrying that assuming isn't the same as saying it.
dialogues.no_response: Alright. I love you. That part doesn't depend on the money. None of the real parts do.

dialogues.text: I'm not going to say goodbye to you today. I want that on the record. I'm only asking for a hundred dollars. That is the entire size of what this is.
dialogues.yes_response: Good. That's all this is. We're not doing anything bigger than this today. I won't let us.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. Okay. I'll be here when you get back. I'll leave a light on. The new lamp, even.
